---
id: "MP141"
title: "Scheduled Execution Pattern"
type: "meta-principle"
category: "data_management"
date_created: "2025-12-23"
date_modified: "2025-12-24"
author: "Claude"
status: "active"
version: "1.1"
weight: 141
priority: "HIGH"
related_to:
  - "MP140": "Pipeline Orchestration Principle - Dependency resolution and execution"
  - "MP142": "Configuration-Driven Pipeline - YAML configuration standard"
  - "MP107": "ETL Pipeline Independence - Parallel execution capability"
  - "MP064": "ETL-Derivation Separation - Physical directory structure"
implements:
  - "DUAL_EXECUTION_MODES": "Manual and scheduled execution support"
  - "OBSERVABILITY": "Status, progress, and visualization capabilities"
  - "FAILURE_RECOVERY": "Retry, resume, and rebuild mechanisms"
  - "LOGGING_AUDIT": "Comprehensive execution logging"
---

# MP141: Scheduled Execution Pattern

## Core Statement

**Pipeline scheduling MUST support dual execution modes (manual and scheduled), provide comprehensive observability, and enable graceful failure recovery with clear logging and audit trails.**

### The Golden Rule of Scheduling

> **"You can always know what ran, when it ran, whether it succeeded, and if it failed, exactly why and how to fix it."**

## Execution Modes

### Mode 1: Manual Execution (On-Demand)

Direct invocation by user or CI/CD system:

```yaml
manual_execution:
  interface: "Makefile CLI"

  commands:
    run_target: "make run TARGET=cbz_D04_02"
    run_platform: "make run PLATFORM=cbz"
    run_all: "make run"
    dry_run: "make dry-run TARGET=cbz_D04_02"

  characteristics:
    - "Immediate execution"
    - "Interactive output to terminal"
    - "Blocking until complete"
    - "Returns exit code for scripting"
```

### Mode 2: Scheduled Execution (Automated)

Periodic execution via system scheduler:

```yaml
scheduled_execution:
  scheduler:
    macos: "launchd (recommended)"
    linux: "systemd timer or cron"

  patterns:
    weekly_full: "Sunday 2:00 AM - Full pipeline refresh"
    daily_incremental: "Daily 6:00 AM - Updated data only"
    on_demand: "Triggered by external event/webhook"

  characteristics:
    - "Background execution"
    - "Output to log files"
    - "Non-blocking"
    - "Notification on completion/failure"
```

## Observability Requirements

### Status Visibility

Every execution MUST provide clear status information:

```yaml
observability:
  status_query:
    command: "make status"
    output:
      - "Current execution state (running/complete/failed)"
      - "Last execution timestamp"
      - "Number of targets up-to-date vs outdated"
      - "Currently executing target (if running)"

  progress_tracking:
    command: "make progress"
    output:
      - "Execution percentage"
      - "Current step N of M"
      - "Estimated time remaining"
      - "Resource utilization"
```

### Dependency Visualization

```yaml
visualization:
  dag_view:
    command: "make vis"
    output: "Interactive DAG visualization in browser"
    shows:
      - "All targets and dependencies"
      - "Execution status (done/pending/running)"
      - "Timestamps for completed targets"

  target_specific:
    command: "make vis TARGET=cbz_D04_02"
    output: "Subgraph for specific target"
    shows:
      - "Only relevant upstream dependencies"
      - "Execution path highlighting"
```

### Logging Standard

```yaml
logging_requirements:
  log_location: "logs/pipeline/"

  file_naming:
    format: "{date}_{time}_{target_or_platform}.log"
    example: "2025-12-23_020000_cbz_full.log"

  log_content:
    required_fields:
      - timestamp: "ISO 8601 format"
      - target: "Script being executed"
      - status: "started|completed|failed|skipped"
      - duration: "Execution time in seconds"
      - exit_code: "0 for success, non-zero for failure"

    on_failure:
      - error_message: "Full error text"
      - stack_trace: "R traceback if available"
      - last_successful_line: "Where execution stopped"
```

## Failure Recovery

### Recovery Mechanisms

```yaml
failure_recovery:
  retry_failed:
    command: "make retry"
    behavior: "Re-run only failed targets"
    use_case: "Transient failures (network, API limits)"

  resume_from:
    command: "make resume"
    behavior: "Continue from last successful point"
    use_case: "Interrupted execution"

  rebuild_target:
    command: "make rebuild TARGET=cbz_D04_02"
    behavior: "Force re-run target and all dependencies"
    use_case: "Corrupted cache or data"

  clean_rebuild:
    command: "make clean && make run"
    behavior: "Destroy all cache, rebuild everything"
    use_case: "Complete refresh needed"
```

### Incremental Execution

```yaml
incremental_execution:
  enabled_by: "targets package caching"

  behavior:
    - "Check if target output exists"
    - "Compare input timestamps to output"
    - "Skip execution if output is newer"
    - "Run only outdated targets"

  benefits:
    - "90%+ time savings on repeat runs"
    - "Only process changed data"
    - "Automatic dependency invalidation"
```

### Failure Isolation

```yaml
failure_isolation:
  principle: "One target failure does not stop independent targets"

  behavior:
    parallel_targets: "Continue other branches on failure"
    sequential_targets: "Stop downstream of failed target"

  notification:
    - "Log failure immediately"
    - "Send alert if configured"
    - "Continue with remaining work"
    - "Report partial success"
```

## Scheduling Configuration

### launchd Configuration (macOS)

```xml
<!-- com.mamba.pipeline.plist -->
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
    <key>Label</key>
    <string>com.mamba.pipeline</string>

    <key>ProgramArguments</key>
    <array>
        <string>/bin/bash</string>
        <string>-c</string>
        <string>cd /path/to/MAMBA/scripts/update_scripts && make run</string>
    </array>

    <key>StartCalendarInterval</key>
    <dict>
        <key>Weekday</key>
        <integer>0</integer>  <!-- Sunday -->
        <key>Hour</key>
        <integer>2</integer>  <!-- 2 AM -->
        <key>Minute</key>
        <integer>0</integer>
    </dict>

    <key>StandardOutPath</key>
    <string>/path/to/logs/pipeline/scheduled.log</string>

    <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
    <string>/path/to/logs/pipeline/scheduled_error.log</string>

    <key>EnvironmentVariables</key>
    <dict>
        <key>PATH</key>
        <string>/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin</string>
    </dict>
</dict>
</plist>
```

### Scheduling Management Commands

```bash
# Set up weekly scheduling
make schedule

# Check if scheduled
make schedule-status

# Disable scheduling
make unschedule

# View scheduled job logs
make schedule-logs
```

## Configuration Management (Pre-Execution)

### Configuration Update Before Scheduled Runs

Per **MP142 Configuration-Driven Pipeline** and **SO_P016 Configuration Scope Hierarchy**, configuration should be regenerated before scheduled execution to capture any changes:

```yaml
configuration_in_scheduling:
  pre_execution_check:
    - "Verify _targets_config.yaml exists"
    - "Check if app_config.yaml changed since last merge"
    - "Optionally regenerate config"

  recommended_workflow:
    manual: "User runs make config-merge before make run"
    scheduled: "Scheduled job can include config-merge"
```

### Scheduled Execution with Config Merge

```bash
# Option 1: Config merge as part of scheduled job
# In launchd plist or cron:
cd /path/to/update_scripts && make config-merge && make run

# Option 2: Separate config update schedule
# Update config daily, run pipeline weekly
```

### Configuration Update Commands

```makefile
# Configuration management in Makefile
config-merge:
    @echo "Merging pipeline configuration..."
    @$(R) -e "source('$(GLOBAL_SCRIPTS)/04_utils/fn_merge_pipeline_config.R'); \
      merge_pipeline_config()"

config-scan:
    @echo "Scanning ETL/DRV directories..."
    @$(R) -e "source('$(GLOBAL_SCRIPTS)/04_utils/fn_merge_pipeline_config.R'); \
      config <- yaml::read_yaml('$(CONFIG_PATH)'); \
      config <- scan_and_update_scripts(config); \
      yaml::write_yaml(config, '$(CONFIG_PATH)')"

config-full:
    @echo "Full configuration: merge + scan + validate"
    $(MAKE) config-merge
    $(MAKE) config-scan
    $(MAKE) config-validate
```

### Configuration in Scheduled Context

```yaml
scheduled_config_management:
  scenario_1_stable:
    description: "Config rarely changes"
    approach: "Skip config-merge in scheduled job"
    when: "Production environment, no active development"

  scenario_2_active:
    description: "Frequent script changes"
    approach: "Include config-merge before every scheduled run"
    when: "Development or active iteration"

  scenario_3_controlled:
    description: "Config changes via CI/CD"
    approach: "CI/CD triggers config-merge on merge to main"
    when: "Enterprise workflow with pull requests"
```

### Three-Layer Config in Scheduling

```yaml
config_layer_scheduling:
  layer_1_universal:
    location: "global_scripts/templates/_targets_config.base.yaml"
    update_frequency: "Only when subrepo updated"
    scheduled_action: "None (read-only)"

  layer_2_company:
    location: "app_config.yaml > pipeline:"
    update_frequency: "When platforms/settings change"
    scheduled_action: "None (manual updates only)"

  layer_3_generated:
    location: "{project_root}/_targets_config.yaml"
    update_frequency: "Before each run (if active development)"
    scheduled_action: "make config-merge (optional)"
```

## Notification Integration

### Notification Channels

```yaml
notifications:
  on_success:
    enabled: false  # Usually not needed
    channels: []

  on_failure:
    enabled: true
    channels:
      - "email"      # Via system mail
      - "slack"      # If webhook configured
      - "log"        # Always to log file

  on_long_running:
    threshold: "30 minutes"
    action: "Log warning"
```

### Notification Content

```yaml
failure_notification:
  subject: "[MAMBA Pipeline] Execution Failed: {target}"

  body_includes:
    - "Failed target name"
    - "Error message"
    - "Last successful target"
    - "Suggested recovery command"
    - "Link to full log"
```

## Resource Management

### Execution Constraints

```yaml
resource_management:
  parallel_limit:
    setting: "MAMBA_WORKERS=4"
    purpose: "Limit concurrent target execution"

  timeout:
    per_target: "30 minutes default"
    total_pipeline: "4 hours default"

  memory_limit:
    monitoring: "Log peak memory usage"
    action: "Warn if approaching limit"
```

### Database Connection Management

```yaml
connection_management:
  pattern: "Per-target connection lifecycle"

  rules:
    - "Each target opens own connections"
    - "Connections closed on target completion"
    - "No shared connections between targets"
    - "Connection pooling handled at R level"
```

## Implementation Guidelines

### Makefile Standard Commands

```makefile
# Required commands for MP141 compliance
.PHONY: run status vis clean retry resume schedule unschedule

# Manual execution
run:
    MAMBA_TARGET=$(TARGET) MAMBA_PLATFORM=$(PLATFORM) \
    Rscript -e "targets::tar_make()"

# Status check
status:
    Rscript -e "targets::tar_progress()"

# Visualization
vis:
    Rscript -e "targets::tar_visnetwork()"

# Clean rebuild
clean:
    Rscript -e "targets::tar_destroy()"

# Retry failed only
retry:
    Rscript -e "targets::tar_make(names = targets::tar_meta(fields = 'error') %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(error)) %>% dplyr::pull(name))"

# Resume from checkpoint
resume:
    Rscript -e "targets::tar_make()"  # targets handles incremental by default

# Scheduling (macOS)
schedule:
    cp scheduling/launchd_template.plist ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.mamba.pipeline.plist
    launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.mamba.pipeline.plist

unschedule:
    launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.mamba.pipeline.plist
    rm -f ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.mamba.pipeline.plist

schedule-status:
    launchctl list | grep mamba.pipeline
```

### Logging Implementation

```r
# Logging wrapper for each target
log_target_execution <- function(target_name, execution_func) {
  start_time <- Sys.time()

  log_entry(list(
    timestamp = format(start_time, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),
    target = target_name,
    status = "started"
  ))

  result <- tryCatch({
    execution_func()
    list(success = TRUE, error = NULL)
  }, error = function(e) {
    list(success = FALSE, error = e$message)
  })

  end_time <- Sys.time()
  duration <- as.numeric(difftime(end_time, start_time, units = "secs"))

  log_entry(list(
    timestamp = format(end_time, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),
    target = target_name,
    status = if (result$success) "completed" else "failed",
    duration = duration,
    error = result$error
  ))

  return(result)
}
```

## Compliance Checklist

### For Pipeline Implementation

```yaml
scheduling_compliance:
  ✓ Makefile provides run, status, vis, clean commands
  ✓ Incremental execution supported (targets caching)
  ✓ Failure does not halt independent targets
  ✓ Logs capture timestamp, target, status, duration
  ✓ retry and resume commands available
  ✓ DAG visualization accessible

  scheduling_specific:
  ✓ launchd plist template provided
  ✓ schedule/unschedule commands work
  ✓ Logs written to standard location
  ✓ Environment variables properly set in scheduled context
```

### For Target Scripts

```yaml
target_compliance:
  ✓ Script handles own initialization
  ✓ Script handles own cleanup
  ✓ Errors produce meaningful messages
  ✓ Script returns success/failure status
  ✓ No interactive prompts (headless compatible)
```

## Benefits Summary

```yaml
scheduling_benefits:
  operational:
    - "Automated data refresh"
    - "Reduced manual intervention"
    - "Consistent execution timing"

  reliability:
    - "Failure isolation"
    - "Automatic retry capability"
    - "Complete audit trail"

  visibility:
    - "Real-time status"
    - "Dependency visualization"
    - "Historical logs"

  efficiency:
    - "Incremental execution"
    - "Resource-aware parallelism"
    - "Skip up-to-date targets"
```

## Conclusion

The Scheduled Execution Pattern ensures MAMBA pipelines are production-ready with:

1. **Dual Modes**: Both manual and scheduled execution supported
2. **Full Observability**: Status, progress, visualization always available
3. **Graceful Recovery**: Retry, resume, rebuild mechanisms
4. **Comprehensive Logging**: Complete audit trail of all executions
5. **Resource Management**: Controlled parallelism and timeouts

**Key Mandates:**
- Every pipeline MUST be executable both manually and via scheduler
- Every execution MUST produce logs with standard fields
- Every failure MUST be recoverable without full rebuild
- Every target MUST be visible in status and visualization
